user.title: Welcome to openSenseMap Advanced
user.description:
Choose how you want to configure your openSenseMap station (senseBox):

• **Pull Mode (Monitor):** Periodically fetch environmental measurements from your station and create sensor entities in Home Assistant.
• **Push Mode (Exporter):** Send local Home Assistant sensor states directly to your openSenseMap Box.

*Tip: You can find your Box ID in the URL of your station's page on [openSenseMap](https://opensensemap.org) (e.g. the alphanumeric string at the end of the URL).*

push_api_key.title: Configure Push API Key
push_api_key.description:
To upload measurements, you need an openSenseMap account and a registered senseBox:

1. Register an account at [opensensemap.org/account/register](https://opensensemap.org/account/register).
2. Register a new senseBox in your dashboard.
3. Under **Hardware**, select your model. **To upload generic Home Assistant sensors** (like Zigbee or ESPHome), choose **Manual configuration** and manually define the sensors you want to export (e.g. title 'Temperatur', unit '°C').
4. Copy the API Key from your openSenseMap profile and paste it below:

push_config.title: Map senseBox Sensors
push_config.description:
Map a local Home Assistant entity to the openSenseMap sensor:

**{sensor_name}** ({sensor_type}) [{sensor_unit}]

Select the local entity to upload, or leave blank to skip:

pull_config.title: Configure Pull Mode (Monitor)
pull_config.description: Set the interval for pulling measurements and whether to fall back on cached values during network drops.
